title: How to mutation to set active , deactive user status?
excerpt:
How to mutation to set active , deactive user status? 
we are using Monday.com GraphQL

posts.author: wannock (wannock)
posts.cookedHtml:
How to mutation to set active , deactive user status?
we are using Monday.com GraphQL

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @wannock - welcome!

At this time it’s not possible to use the API to change the active users status.

You can see a full list of possible mutations on the API right now from our documentation, though we’re always working on expanding this.

-Daniel

posts.author: rikita.poojari (Rikita Poojari)
posts.cookedHtml:
@dsilva
Is there any plan to add this API for User mutation in future?
Can a feature request be submitted to prioritise this?

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @rikita.poojari!

Sadly not at this time. I have submitted this as feedback for our team’s consideration but I imagine this API change could stir up a whole host of other problems relating to permissions settings and such.

Happy to try either way.

posts.author: bmack (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml: User API mutations, to include modifying a user status, needs to be supported. It is ridiculous to have to manually edit users one at a time vs an API, script or bulk action.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @bmack and welcome to the community!

I hope you like it here

Thank you for that feedback.

I will make sure our team gets it so it can be reviewed.

Cheers,
Matias
